summaries.144165: Root cause confirmed as a use-build-context-synchronously violation: spellcheck code uses a BuildContext after an async gap, causing 'widget has been unmounted' crashes in production Crashlytics logs. Comments provide a reliable reproduction sample (append/delete a character, wait a second). One commenter applied a user-side workaround filtering the specific exception in FlutterError.onError. Labeled for further investigation; no fix PR linked.
summaries.38825: Confirmed on iOS, Android, and web (mobile Chrome) including endDrawer, across stable 3.3 and master 3.7. Drawer is not a child of Scaffold so resizeToAvoidBottomInset does not apply. Two workarounds: (1) wrap Drawer content in a Column with SizedBox(height: MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom), (2) use Scaffold as Drawer child with resizeToAvoidBottomInset: true. No fix PR linked.
summaries.105028: Root cause identified: Flutter uses Material/Cupertino translations generated via Translation Console rather than Apple-platform-native strings; Android uses '翻訳 切り取り コピー 取り付け' while iOS users expect '翻訳 カット コピー ペースト'. Fixing Cupertino ARB strings would address iOS/macOS; a separate string set is needed for Windows/Linux/Android since the underlying string is shared. No fix PR linked; stalled.
summaries.50329: Confirmed on Android. Scrollable.ensureVisible resolves immediately and does not account for keyboard animation delay, so it cannot replicate EditableText's scroll-into-view behavior. A workaround with a FocusNode listener plus Timer delay was proposed but deemed unsuitable for production. No fix PR linked; stalled.
summaries.94502: Initially thought to be remote-desktop-specific but confirmed on native Windows 10/11 with Flutter 3.3+ for multiple keys (Insert, Shift, Alt-Left). Root cause: key state desync — KeyRepeatEvent or KeyDownEvent dispatched when _pressedKeys shows inconsistent state. Also reported on Android TV with a physical remote. Key fuzzing suggested. Affects stable 3.3.x through 3.16.x; triaged P4, team-desktop label removed during re-triage. No fix PR.
summaries.90525: Root cause partially identified by bisect: commit 136da59e6b47eac6f2c0cdfaf376bd755b46690a (text editing actions) introduced the regression. Samsung keyboard sends backspace as a hardware KeyMessage rather than through TextInputPlugin, bypassing the deletion path. Workaround: wrap widget tree with DefaultTextEditingShortcuts; a second workaround is wrapping TextFormField in Material(type: MaterialType.transparency). Samsung never responded. Confirmed on keyboard versions 5.2–5.3; Gboard unaffected. Stalled, no fix PR.
summaries.122300: Root cause identified by @cbracken: changing obscureText while a TextInputConnection is open does not call TextInput.updateConfig to sync with the embedder, affecting all platforms. Engine PR #39460 fixed the initial-obscureText case but not mid-session toggling. Workaround: set AutofillHints.password (activates Android Autofill Service, which may be undesirable). Proposed fix: sync all TextInputConfiguration fields to engine on any change; @cbracken had a macOS engine implementation ready. No cherry-pick to stable planned.
summaries.127947: Comments clarify this is expected behavior per Flutter's InheritedWidget semantics: MediaQuery.of(context) creates a dependency that triggers rebuilds on any viewInsets change (keyboard animation). PR #114459 introduced selective MediaQuery aspects (MediaQuery.sizeOf) as a mitigation. Suggested workarounds: use MediaQuery.sizeOf/viewInsetsOf or LayoutBuilder. Team member suggested closing as won't-fix with possible docs update; issue stayed open with user follow-up through Flutter 3.24.0.
summaries.118558: Narrowed to Flutter web in an iframe on Android Chrome physical devices. After scrolling in a ListView or SingleChildScrollView the scrollable area loses gesture hit-testing; sibling widgets outside the scroll area remain interactive. Workaround: remove Flutter from the iframe entirely. No fix PR; stalled with no ETA.
summaries.138207: Comments reveal that FocusNode.nextFocus() moves focus to the node after the currently focused node, not after the given FocusNode — matching documented behavior. User expected it to advance relative to the specified FocusNode regardless of current focus. Issue confirmed specific to TextField (TextButton behaves differently because buttons don't request focus on click). Assigned to @gspencergoog then unassigned due to no progress; reflects API-design gap rather than a clear bug.
summaries.148604: Confirmed on iOS 17.4 (iPhone 15 Pro) and cross-platform. iOS and Android produce oldText delta of '. run tom' while web produces '.' and macOS shows '.' with no newline appended. Related to #129061 and #142894. No fix or root cause analysis beyond platform-specific delta behavior identified in comments.
summaries.95656: Confirmed on physical iOS devices only (not simulator) across stable 2.8.1 through 3.10.5; swipe-back gesture triggers keyboard while hardware back button does not. Root cause: didStartUserGesture causes FocusNode from prior page to become primaryFocus. Workarounds: (1) NavigatorObserver calling FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus() in didStartUserGesture, (2) FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus() in GoRouter redirect. No fix PR; issue persists.
summaries.106767: Root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong: Container color changing from null to non-null restructures the widget tree (inserts ColoredBox), causing TextField to be removed and re-inflated; the new EditableText does not handle being given an already-focused FocusNode in initState. Workarounds: replace null color with Colors.transparent, use GlobalKey on TextField, use ColoredBox instead of Container. Assigned to @xu-baolin who stated intent to fix but made no progress; unassigned. No fix PR.
summaries.133433: Narrowed to physical Android + Chrome devices; emulators and iOS Safari unaffected. When resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false, Chrome Android resizes the browser viewport outside Flutter's control, pushing content up. Workaround: use kIsWeb ? 0 : MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom as bottom padding (partially mitigates). Assigned then unassigned to @htoor3 due to inactivity; confirmed still reproducible on stable and master through at least 2024. No fix PR landed.
summaries.122443: Confirmed Android-only (simulator and device); iOS does not trigger the assertion. The failed assertion in _updateComposingRectIfNeeded was subsequently removed due to false positives per #110343. @Renzo-Olivares reproduced on master with Android simulator but could not write a failing test, suggesting the root cause may be engine-side. A downstream user reported related grey-screen crashes from caret set beyond end of text. Underlying positioning issue unresolved after assertion removal.
